data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T154152-4a9c452c/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T154152-4a9c452c/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T154152-4a9c452c/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T154152-4a9c452c/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T154152-4a9c452c/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-4ab66676">
    <title>cli: translate SIGINT/SIGTERM into graceful ddx cancellation</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Ctrl-C / SIGTERM should trigger DDx cooperative cancellation for `ddx work` and `ddx try`, but the CLI root currently executes without a signal-backed context. Operators see abrupt termination rather than an explicit shutdown message and cleanup path.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/main.go:31 calls `cmd.Execute(workingDir)` and relies on the default process signal behavior.
- cli/cmd/root.go:31-39 initializes the root command and calls `rootCmd.Execute()` instead of `ExecuteContext(ctx)`.
- cli/cmd/work.go:37-40 advertises `signal` as a stop condition, but no production signal handler cancels `cmd.Context()`.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:423-434 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:177-190 require first-signal cooperative shutdown and the exact operator message `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Install a root SIGINT/SIGTERM handler for production CLI execution using `signal.NotifyContext` or equivalent.
- Execute Cobra with the signal-backed context.
- On first signal, print `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully` exactly once to the operator-visible stream before cancellation-dependent cleanup begins.
- On a second signal, hard-abort or restore default signal behavior so wedged cleanup can still be killed.
- Keep tests deterministic by exposing a small helper/seam rather than sending real OS signals where unit tests can use a cancellable context.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not decide bead cleanup policy here beyond propagating context cancellation.
- Do not change server worker stop/watchdog behavior.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Production root command execution uses a SIGINT/SIGTERM-backed context and calls ExecuteContext.
2. First signal prints exactly `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully` once, visible to the operator.
3. Second signal hard-aborts or restores default signal behavior; the behavior is documented in a source comment and covered by a focused test where practical.
4. TestRootSignal_FirstInterruptCancelsContextAndPrintsGracefulMessage verifies cancellation and exact message without relying on slow real-time sleeps.
5. TestRootSignal_MessagePrintedOnce verifies repeated first-path cancellation does not duplicate the message.
6. Existing command tests that use ExecuteContext continue to control cancellation explicitly.
7. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run "TestRootSignal|TestWork|TestTry|TestExecuteLoop" -count=1` passes.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:cli, area:agent, kind:bug, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025, interrupt</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-99a3be32" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="e14178f31d037acef34ece3b2e662a36ac97dc2c" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T154152-4a9c452c"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

out=1.7KB 26 lines "│  Skipping hook sync: core.hooksPath is set ..."

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `79aa17c62` (`fix: add graceful root signal handling [ddx-4ab66676]`).

What changed:
- [cli/cmd/root.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4ab66676-20260506T154152-4a9c452c/cli/cmd/root.go#L38) now runs the root command under `signal.NotifyContext` for `SIGINT`/`SIGTERM`, calls `ExecuteContext` through the helper, and maps `context.Canceled` to an `ExitError` with code `130`.
- [cli/cmd/root_signal.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4ab66676-20260506T154152-4a9c452c/cli/cmd/root_signal.go#L12) adds the one-shot graceful shutdown announcer and the root execution helper. The source comment documents that `stop()` is called on first cancel so a second signal falls back to default behavior.
- [cli/cmd/root_signal_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4ab66676-20260506T154152-4a9c452c/cli/cmd/root_signal_test.go#L14) adds:
  - `TestRootSignal_FirstInterruptCancelsContextAndPrintsGracefulMessage`
  - `TestRootSignal_MessagePrintedOnce`

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestRootSignal|TestWork|TestTry|TestExecuteLoop' -count=1`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`

Worktree is clean after the commit.
